title: SMBv1 Enabled and SMB Signing Not Required
description: Samba 3.0.20-Debian has SMBv1 enabled and SMB signing is not required. SMBv1 is vulnerable to multiple critical exploits (EternalBlue, etc.) and lack of SMB signing enables NTLM relay attacks. 35 user accounts were enumerated via null session including root, msfadmin, user, service, postgres, and other service accounts.
evidence:
SMB Configuration Analysis:
- SMBv1 protocol is enabled (CRITICAL)
- SMB signing is not required (HIGH)
- Samba version: 3.0.20-Debian
- Domain: localdomain (WORKGROUP)
- OS: METASPLOITABLE

Null Session User Enumeration (35 accounts):
games, nobody, bind, proxy, syslog, user, www-data, root, news, postgres, bin, mail, distccd, proftpd, dhcp, daemon, sshd, man, lp, mysql, gnats, libuuid, backup, msfadmin, telnetd, sys, klog, postfix, service, list, irc, ftp, tomcat55, sync, uucp
remediation: Disable SMBv1 protocol. Enable and require SMB signing on all SMB connections. Upgrade Samba to a current supported version. Disable null session enumeration. Restrict SMB access to authorized hosts.

issues: HIGH severity finding should have stronger evidence
Exploitation proof required for high/critical findings
suggestions: Add command output demonstrating access or data extraction
For high findings, demonstrate actual impact
Actually exploit the vulnerability and capture output
